summary:
Glanzmann disease results from a genetic mutation leading to defective or absent glycoprotein IIb/IIIa receptors on platelets, impairing their ability to aggregate and form clots effectively. This condition causes prolonged bleeding due to impaired primary hemostasis at sites of vascular injury.

The summary above correctly identifies that this is not related to factor deficiencies but instead stems directly from an intrinsic problem with platelet structure/function—specifically involving GPIIb-IIIa—and emphasizes its impact on initial blood coagulation through disrupted platelet adhesion/aggregation during vessel damage.

The summary above correctly identifies that this is not related to factor deficiencies but instead stems directly from an intrinsic problem with platelet structure/function—specifically involving GPIIb-IIIa—and emphasizes its impact on initial blood coagulation through disrupted platelet adhesion/aggregation during vessel damage.. Answer choice: A
